markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has locked Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 console launch following two delays for additional polish, with Take-Two Interactive reaffirming the date and signaling that a major marketing push will begin this summer. Traders monitoring the “before GTA VI” market focus on Rockstar’s pattern of cryptic social media drops and official trailers, alongside ongoing GTA Online seasonal updates that could serve as the final pre-launch content cadence. Summer 2026 earnings calls and any early promotional assets will likely move implied probabilities fastest, as the studio historically builds momentum through controlled reveals rather than broad campaigns. The five-month window to release leaves room for one or two significant teases but limits major surprises.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials and stalled retrials in Weinstein’s New York cases have driven the overwhelming market consensus toward no additional prison time. After a split verdict in the 2025 retrial produced one conviction and an acquittal, the final rape charge ended in a May 2026 deadlock, prompting prosecutors to weigh a potential fourth trial with a June hearing looming. Weinstein’s preexisting California sentence and appeals process create uncertainty around consecutive time, while his age, health complaints, and prolonged Rikers detention have fueled trader views that further meaningful incarceration is unlikely. Upcoming prosecutorial decisions and any plea developments remain the key near-term catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes hold a commanding 79.5% implied probability in the 2026 Stanley Cup market after building a 3-2 series lead over the Vegas Golden Knights in the Final, driven by their league-best 113-point regular season, Eastern Conference dominance, and 12-1 playoff record entering the championship round. Carolina's depth, strong home performance, and recent 4-2 Game 5 victory have solidified trader consensus around their path to the title. Vegas retains a 20% chance through demonstrated resilience, including multiple overtime wins and a competitive series split, though injuries like the absence of key contributors and the need for a must-win Game 6 limit their upside. The wisdom of crowds reflected in current pricing accounts for these momentum and situational factors heading into the decisive games.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 82% implied probability to claim the 2026 NBA title after seizing a 3-1 lead in the Finals against the San Antonio Spurs. Their commanding position stems from a record-setting 29-point comeback victory in Game 4 at Madison Square Garden, which erased a massive deficit and shifted momentum decisively. The Knicks, who posted a 53-29 regular-season mark and advanced through the Eastern Conference playoffs, now sit one win from their first championship since 1973. Meanwhile, the Spurs (62-20 in the regular season) trail despite strong contributions from Victor Wembanyama, with their +380 odds reflecting the steep challenge of winning three consecutive games to force a Game 7. Trader consensus prices in the historical difficulty of such comebacks while acknowledging the Spurs' remaining home-court opportunities.

Meanwhile, the Spurs (62-20 in the regular season) trail despite strong contributions from Victor Wembanyama, with their +380 odds reflecting the steep challenge of winning three consecutive games to force a Game 7. Trader consensus prices in the historical difficulty of such comebacks while acknowledging the Spurs' remaining home-court opportunities.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and the People's Liberation Army as of mid-2026, with analysts widely expecting him to secure a fourth term as General Secretary at the 21st Party Congress in autumn 2027. Recent military leadership purges, including the removal of senior figures such as Zhang Youxia, have eliminated potential internal challengers and further centralized authority around Xi loyalists, reducing prospects for elite-level opposition or sudden removal. No verified signs of health issues, factional revolts, or policy reversals have emerged to threaten his position before the end of 2026. Speculation about successors or post-Xi transitions focuses on appointments at or after the 2027 congress, with credible reporting indicating that broader discontent has not translated into organized challenges under prevailing party discipline and surveillance mechanisms. This environment underpins the strong trader consensus favoring continuity through the resolution window.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 23% in the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market, reflecting his high national visibility as term-limited California governor and recent positioning through public opposition to the Trump administration alongside populist shifts on AI regulation and affordability. A fragmented field follows, with Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Jon Ossoff, and Kamala Harris clustered near 9%, drawing from legislative records, Senate experience, and prior national exposure. Key differentiators include governing records versus ideological or congressional profiles, plus varying capacity to consolidate moderate and progressive factions or perform with key voting blocs ahead of the 2026 midterms. Support could consolidate behind one contender through strong midterm results, early fundraising edges, or polling momentum in battleground states, though no formal declarations have occurred.
